chapter_context.summary: Presents the famous Song of the Vineyard, portraying Israel as a failed vineyard that produced wild grapes, followed by a series of six 'woes' against social injustice.

outline.text: The Song of the Failed Vineyard and Wild Grapes
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Song of the Failed Vineyard and Wild Grapes (5:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: The Six Woes Against Covetousness, Drunkenness, Pride, and Social Injustice
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Six Woes Against Covetousness, Drunkenness, Pride, and Social Injustice (5:8-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: The Outstretching of God's Anger and the Invasion of the Swift Nation
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Outstretching of God's Anger and the Invasion of the Swift Nation (5:24-30), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 5, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
